title: Fungal infection
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Defines dermatophyte subtypes but focuses on tinea pedis, including itch, scale, fissures, maceration, erythema, vesicles, ulcers, and foot distribution.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped tinea-pedis or dermatophyte terminology, itch, annular or scaly description, foot distribution, contact context, and compatible lesion findings only in its stated subtype.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Defines dermatophyte subtypes but focuses on tinea pedis, including itch, scale, fissures, maceration, erythema, vesicles, ulcers, and foot distribution.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Tinea Pedis (Athlete’s Foot)

red_flags.text: Lists severe pain, hot red foot, diabetes, immunosuppression, nail or body spread, secondary infection, systemic illness, extensive disease, and non-response.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped fever, spreading redness, pain, discharge, face or neck spread, extensive disease, immunocompromise, secondary infection, non-response, and worsening fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists severe pain, hot red foot, diabetes, immunosuppression, nail or body spread, secondary infection, systemic illness, extensive disease, and non-response.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Tinea Pedis (Athlete’s Foot)

investigations.text: Describes clinician-selected microscopy, fungal culture, bacterial culture, and rare biopsy when diagnosis is uncertain or atypical.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed scraping, microscopy or KOH, fungal culture, and previous-treatment fields.
investigations.evidence_extract: Describes clinician-selected microscopy, fungal culture, bacterial culture, and rare biopsy when diagnosis is uncertain or atypical.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Tinea Pedis (Athlete’s Foot)

medication.text: Covers clinician-led hygiene, reinfection prevention, topical or systemic treatment choices, and reassessment; no dose-bearing content is mapped.
medication.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-documented hygiene, recurrence prevention, medicine decisions, questions, return, and follow-up without doses.
medication.evidence_extract: Covers clinician-led hygiene, reinfection prevention, topical or systemic treatment choices, and reassessment; no dose-bearing content is mapped.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Tinea Pedis (Athlete’s Foot)

source_limitations: Broad fungal or candidal identity, ringworm beyond compatible descriptions, groin, body, scalp, candidiasis, contact spread, fixed two-to-four-week timing, and non-foot treatment remain unsupported.
Tinea or candidiasis remains clinician-stated; this subtype source must not be treated as broad fungal diagnosis coverage.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
